findings.description: PDF page 1 is embedded as a screenshot image within the HTML in addition to being converted to text. Rendering an entire PDF page as a single screenshot is a conversion failure.
findings.evidence: HTML chunk 1 contains a reduced-size screenshot of the full PDF page 1 (showing the seal, letterhead, body text, and DEQ footer) embedded as an image alongside the text conversion of the same content.

findings.description: Page 1 content appears twice in the HTML: once as a screenshot image and once as converted HTML text. This duplication interrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence: In HTML chunk 1, the full page 1 appears as a thumbnail screenshot image, immediately followed by the same content rendered as HTML text (ROY COOPER, Governor, subject line, body paragraphs, bullet list, etc.).

findings.description: PDF page 2 is embedded as a screenshot image within the HTML in addition to being converted to text.
findings.evidence: HTML chunk 2-3 contains a reduced-size screenshot of PDF page 2 (showing the continuation about emission limits, appendices list, and public comment period details) embedded as an image alongside the text conversion.

findings.description: Page 2 content appears twice in the HTML: once as a screenshot image and once as converted HTML text, creating reading order disruption.
findings.evidence: In HTML chunks 2-3, a screenshot of page 2 is embedded between text sections, so the same appendix lists (B-3, F-1, F-4, G-1, G-2, G-3, H, I) and public comment information appear both in the image and in the surrounding text.

findings.description: PDF page 3 is embedded as a screenshot image within the HTML in addition to being converted to text.
findings.evidence: HTML chunks 3-4 contain a reduced-size screenshot of PDF page 3 (showing the closing paragraphs, signature, and cc list) embedded as an image alongside the text conversion.

findings.description: Page 3 content appears twice in the HTML: once as a screenshot image and once as converted HTML text, creating reading order disruption.
findings.evidence: In HTML chunks 3-4, a screenshot of page 3 (including signature block, MAA/rps, Enclosures, and cc list) is embedded alongside the same content rendered as HTML text, causing the reader to encounter the closing, signature, and cc list twice.

findings.description: Pages 2 and 3 of the PDF are rendered BOTH as full-page embedded images AND as extracted HTML text, duplicating all content from those pages.
findings.evidence: HTML contains followed by the same text content (appendix list, public notice paragraphs, etc.) as HTML paragraphs. Similarly, is followed by the same closing paragraphs and cc list as HTML text.

findings.description: Two full-page scan images are included that do not correspond to distinct substantive images in the PDF (they are redundant page scans). Meanwhile, the NC DEQ logo at the bottom of page 1 of the PDF is not captured as a separate image.
findings.evidence: HTML image 2 alt='Page 2 of the letter from the North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality' and image 3 alt='Page 3 of the letter, including the signature of Michael A. Abraczinskas' are full-page captures rather than discrete images (seal, logo, signature). The footer logo/seal on page 1 of the PDF is not represented.
